input.background: We want to compare the taste preferences of consumers for three different brands of bottled water (Brands A, B, and C) using a random sample of 15 bottled water consumers. The goal is to set up a completely randomized design for this purpose, assigning experimental units (consumers) to treatments (brands).
input.question: How can we set up a completely randomized design for this study?

output.answer: One way to set up the completely randomized design is to randomly assign one of the three brands to each consumer to taste. Then we could measure (say, on a 1-to-10-point scale) the taste preference of each consumer.

meta_info.caption: Assigning Treatments in a Completely Randomized Design-Bottled Water Brands Study
meta_info.chapter: Design of Experiments and Analysis of Variance
meta_info.section: 9.2 The Completely Randomized Design: Single Factor
